bead.title: Quarantine unrepairable Quill segments and synchronously restore lexical freshness
bead.implemented_lane: opt-in durable writer quarantine coupled to fsfs freshness audit and reindex

quarantine_contract.retention: segment and repair sidecar are renamed to collision-safe .quarantine artifacts and never deleted
publish_order: corrupt source and sidecar are durably renamed
a successor MANIFEST omitting the quarantined segment is published
quarantine_contract.reopen_behavior: every open discovers retained quarantine artifacts, emits a typed warning, and exposes degraded metadata
quarantine_contract.missing_estimate: known document ranges are reported when available; aged-out ranges are counted explicitly as unknown

freshness_repair_contract.watch_start: the paired manifest freshness audit runs synchronously before the watcher starts
freshness_repair_contract.repair: source text is recanonicalized and lexical plus vector membership is restored without duplicating the already-certified catalog transaction
freshness_repair_contract.postcondition: the post-repair audit must be clean before watch startup proceeds
freshness_repair_contract.vector_membership: freshness inspection includes both compacted FSVI records and resident WAL entries

degraded_surface.search_json: index_freshness includes all quarantine degradation fields
degraded_surface.doctor: reports quarantine count and known or unknown missing-document estimates with remediation guidance
degraded_surface.telemetry: quill.keeper.segment_quarantined is emitted with the segment id, retained path, estimate, and mandatory-audit marker

proves: opt-in writer open quarantines corrupt FSLX and .fec
successor snapshot excludes the corrupt segment
quarantine bytes remain on disk and are included in managed disk accounting
production Quill segment protection and corruption
search-only JSON and doctor degraded surfacing
one-shot rebuild can delete stale Quill rows after a staged upsert
the lexical backend commits any dirty scalar generation before delete

observed: 1836 other frankensearch-fsfs tests passed and one was ignored
one unrelated updater test failed to execute a just-written shell fixture with Linux ETXTBSY

sole_failure.error: Os { code: 26, kind: ExecutableFileBusy, message: "Text file busy" }

invalidated_diagnostics.observation: the first workspace test attempt did not compile because a storage batch context was retained across the spawned CLI future
invalidated_diagnostics.resolution: the quarantine reindex path now performs only lexical and vector membership restoration; paired manifests already certify the storage transaction
invalidated_diagnostics.authority: superseded by the passing workspace check, Clippy gate, and targeted end-to-end test

invalidated_diagnostics.observation: the first exact-generation rerun still failed after committing only same-flush buffered documents
invalidated_diagnostics.resolution: QuillLexicalBackend now checks the Quill index's actual dirty state and commits before every delete
invalidated_diagnostics.authority: superseded by the passing production-shaped targeted test and its pass in the workspace run

workspace_cargo_fmt.detail: RCH rejected cargo fmt --check with RCH-E301 because it is not a remote compilation command. No local Cargo fallback was used; direct nightly rustfmt is authoritative.

ubs.detail: UBS reported that TOML and Markdown are unsupported and checked nothing. Rust UBS was not run because it launches hidden local Cargo subchecks, which are inadmissible under the strict remote-only policy.

agent_mail.detail: The Agent Mail corruption circuit breaker opened on a malformed database. No unauthorized repair was attempted; Git, Beads, and isolated-worktree state remained authoritative.

remote_only.note: Every authoritative Cargo command ran through RCH with RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1.
